diagrams.title: A* Algorithm Evaluation Function
diagrams.description: Shows how A* calculates the priority of nodes using f(n) = g(n) + h(n), where g(n) is the actual cost from start to node n, and h(n) is the heuristic estimate from n to goal. This visual breakdown helps understand why A* is both optimal and efficient.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the core evaluation function that drives node selection in A* search

diagrams.title: A* Search Process Step-by-Step
diagrams.description: Illustrates the sequential expansion of nodes in A* search on a sample graph. Shows OPEN and CLOSED lists, node expansion order, and how the algorithm finds the optimal path by always selecting the node with lowest f(n) value.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how A* systematically explores and prioritizes nodes to find the optimal path

diagrams.title: Best-First vs A* Comparison
diagrams.description: Contrasts pure Best-First Search (using only h(n)) with A* (using g(n)+h(n)). Shows how A* avoids the pitfall of Best-First getting trapped in suboptimal paths by considering actual path cost alongside heuristic estimate.
diagrams.purpose: Explains why A* combines both cost and heuristic, and why this leads to guaranteed optimality
